title: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
tagline: Five ingredients. Fifteen minutes. Italian perfection.
description: Spaghetti tossed in garlic-infused olive oil with red pepper flakes and parsley. The simplest pasta that proves less is more.
steps: Cook spaghetti in well-salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat.
Add sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook slowly 4-5 minutes, stirring often, until garlic is light golden. Don't let it brown.
Remove skillet from heat when garlic is ready.
Add drained pasta directly to the skillet. Toss with 1/2 cup pasta water.
Add parsley and salt. Toss vigorously to emulsify the oil and pasta water into a silky sauce.
Serve immediately with more parsley and parmesan if desired.
notes: Low and slow garlic: This is the whole dish. Burnt garlic = bitter dish. Take your time.
Pasta water: The starch is essential for creating the sauce. Don't skip it.
Good olive oil: You'll taste it. Use the best you have.
Add protein: Anchovies, shrimp, or breadcrumbs are traditional additions.
Technique: The tossing action emulsifies the oil and water. Keep the pasta moving.
